courses.description: A behind-the-scenes overview of the computational/algorithmic principles that form the basis of today's digital society. Exploration areas include social media, web search, videogames and location-based services.

sections.description: This is a GE-F Quantitative Reasoning course for NON-MAJORS. No credit will be awarded to CSCI, CECS, CSGA, or CSBA majors.

courses.name: Explorations in Computing

courses.description: Fundamental concepts of algorithmic thinking as a primer to programming. Introduction to C++.
courses.name: Introduction to Programming

courses.description: Basic datatypes, assignments, control statements (if, switch, for, while), input/output (printf, scanf, cin, cout), functions, arrays, structures, recursion, dynamic memory, file handling. Programming in C/C++.

sections.description: Students must complete CSCI 102, or pass its challenge exam, or have AP Computer Science A credit to enroll in this course.

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming

courses.description: Data structures including linked lists, balanced search trees, heaps, hash tables; probability and number theory applicable to the analysis of randomized data structures.
courses.name: Data Structures

courses.description: Introduction to Python and C++ with examples from various domains in computational science; intended for students without prior programming experience.
courses.name: Programming Fundamentals for Computing

courses.description: Solutions to computational science problems using Python and C++; data structures; searching and sorting algorithms; computational efficiency.
courses.name: Programming for Applications of Computing

courses.description: Boolean algebra, propositional and first-order logic, proof techniques. Sets, functions, series, summations. Big-O notation; algorithm analysis. Recursion; basic sorting algorithms.  Graphs and basic graph algorithms.
courses.name: Discrete Methods in Computer Science

courses.description: Object-Oriented Programming in Java; using Java threads, Web Services and SQL to write client/server applications; team project; vibe coding with AI tools.
courses.name: Principles of Software Development

courses.description: Algorithm analysis. Greedy algorithms, divide and conquer, dynamic programming, graph algorithms. NP-completeness and basic recursion theory and undecidability. Sorting lower bounds. Number-theory based cryptography.
courses.name: Introduction to Algorithms and Theory of Computing

courses.description: Introduction to the software engineering process and software lifecycle. Covers project management, requirements, architecture, design, implementation, testing, and maintenance phase activities in team based projects. Prerequisites: CSCI 201. Duplicates credit in former CSCI 377.
courses.name: Software Engineering

courses.description: Basic issues in concurrency, deadlock control, synchronization scheduling, memory management, protection and access control, inter-process communication, and structured design. Laboratory experiences with Unix-like operating system.
courses.name: Fundamentals of Operating Systems

courses.description: Global Internet: design principles, layering, protocol design/analysis. Networked applications, Internet structure/architecture, Protocols for transport/congestion control, network layer/routing, link layer/MAC. Network security.
courses.name: Internetworking

courses.description: Computer organization; entity abstraction and representation; program execution; code optimization; memory usage; exception handling; processing control; computer performance; hands-on work done in C and assembly.
courses.name: Computer Systems

courses.description: Concepts and algorithms underlying the understanding and construction of intelligent systems. Agents, problem solving, search, representation, reasoning, planning, machine learning.
courses.name: Artificial Intelligence: Principles and Foundations

courses.description: Programming applications with dynamic graphical user interfaces. Topics include events, controls, resources, data bindings, styles and user experience.
courses.name: Cross-Platform App Development with Flutter

courses.description: Underlying concepts and principles required for programming video games (topics include vectors, transformations, 3-D math, geometric primitives, matrices).
courses.name: Video Game Programming

courses.description: Group project with an outside stakeholder to develop real-world    software solutions to large-scale problems. Topics include         software engineering, professional preparation, and recent         computer science research. Duplicates credit in CSCI 477a and CSCI  477b.

sections.notes: This course is only open to undergraduate students. No exceptions.
sections.description: This course is only open to undergraduate students. No exceptions.

sections.notes: This course is only open to undergraduate students. No exceptions.
sections.description: This course is only open to undergraduate students. No exceptions.

courses.name: Capstone: Design and Construction of Large       Software Systems

courses.description: Concurrency, deadlock control, synchronization, process and thread scheduling, memory management, file systems, security and access   control, communication and networking, distributed file systems,   data management. Duplicates credit in CSCI 350.
courses.recommendedPrep: CSCI 201 or CSCI 455; CSCI 356 or EE 352

sections.description: CSCI 402 is open only to graduate students.
CSCI 402 is open only to graduate students.

courses.name: Operating Systems

courses.description: Computer graphics, OpenGL, 2D and 3D transformations, Bzier splines, computer animation, rendering including ray tracing, shading and lighting, artistic rendering, virtual reality, visualization.
courses.name: Computer Graphics

courses.description: Developing games or technology based on current and relevant special topics.
courses.name: Game Prototyping

courses.description: A broad overview of security threats and defenses, security systems and functionalities. Includes homeworks and in-class exercises to provide practical experience working with such systems. Prerequisite: CSCI 104.
courses.name: Computer and Network Security

courses.description: An introduction to alternative programming paradigms including functional programming and logic programming.
courses.name: Principles of Functional Programming

courses.description: Applications of advanced concepts in C++ including lambda expressions, templates, secure coding, parallel programming, writing performant code, CMake and continuous integration.
courses.name: Professional C++

courses.description: Network architectures; layered protocols, network service interface; local, wide area, wireless networks; Internet protocols; link protocols; addressing; routing; flow control; software defined network; multimedia networks.
courses.name: Foundations of Computer Networks

courses.description: Introduction to parallel programming techniques, models and optimization strategies; Application mapping to multi-core, accelerator, GPU and cloud platforms; High Performance Computing and Data Science applications.
courses.name: Parallel and Distributed Computation

courses.description: Design flow, tools, and issues related to System/Network-on-Chip (S/Noc) design for real-time embedded systems with applications in mobile, cloud, aerospace, and medical electronics.
courses.name: System-on-Chip

courses.description: Intensive introduction to programming principles, discrete mathematics for computing, software design and software engineering concepts. Not available for credit to computer sciencemajors, graduate or undergraduate.
courses.name: Introduction to Programming Systems Design

courses.description: Register Transfer level machine organization; performance; arithmetic; pipelined processors; exceptions, out-of-order and speculative execution, cache, virtual memory, multi-core multi-threaded processors, cache coherence.
courses.name: Computer Systems Organization

courses.description: Rounding errors in digital computation; solution of linear algebraic systems; Newton's method for nonlinear systems; matrix eigenvalues; polynomial approximation; numerical integration; numerical solution of ordinary differential equations.

sections.description: Register for 1 lec & 1 dis listed immediately following that lec

courses.name: Numerical Methods

courses.description: Hands-on AI: data mining, machine learning, optimization and fairness in the context of applications with environmental and societal benefit.
courses.recommendedPrep: Python programming skills. Courses that use Python (depending on instructor) include: CSCI 113, CSCI 353, CSCI 360, CSCI 445, EE 250, EE 364, TAC 115, and TAC 116.

sections.notes: MS and Non-CS BS Student Clearance Request: https://forms.gle/2KpUvfdJZju8taoM8
sections.description: MS and Non-CS BS Student Clearance Request: https://forms.gle/2KpUvfdJZju8taoM8

courses.name: Artificial Intelligence for Sustainable Development

courses.description: Algorithms for learning behavior from data, including both mathematical underpinnings and practical techniques. Covers supervised learning, deep learning, unsupervised learning and reinforcement learning.
courses.recommendedPrep: CSCI 360, comfortable with mathematical derivations, such as those in MATH 225; comfortable with manipulating vectors and matrices
courses.name: Foundations of Machine Learning

courses.description: Individual research and readings. Not available for graduate credit.
courses.name: Directed Research

courses.description: Design, iterative prototyping, and development of a 1st playable level.
courses.name: Advanced Game Project

courses.description: An original project will be constructed applying computer technology (in either hardware or software) to produce a result useful in the physics classroom or laboratory.
courses.name: Senior Project

courses.description: Computational linear algebra; solution of general nonlinear       systems of equations; approximation theory using functional        analysis; numerical solution of ordinary and partial differential  equations.
courses.name: Numerical Analysis

courses.description: Probability spaces; properties of probability and expectation; discrete and continuous random variables; indicator method; conditioning; transformations; laws of large numbers; Central Limit Theorem without measure theory
courses.name: Applied Probability

courses.description: Components, software and applications of cyber-physical systems; autonomy; control techniques; development and testing; artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms.
courses.recommendedPrep: Fundamentals of control and automata theory; familiarity with Matlab, Simulink, and R
courses.name: Autonomous Cyber-Physical Systems

courses.description: Explore the complex engineering process required to design and build a real-time graphics engine to support physical realism on mobile devices. Recommended preparation: CSCI 420 or CSCI 580 or an equivalent course in graphics.
courses.name: Advanced Mobile Devices and Game Consoles

courses.description: Team projects intended to address the multifaceted technical and creative challenges that are inherent to comprehensive game development.
courses.name: Graduate Advanced Game Project

courses.description: Protecting computer networks and systems using cryptography,  authentication, authorization, intrusion detection and response.   Includes lab to provide practical experience working with such  systems.

sections.description: The pre-req of CSCI 402 is automatically waived for CS MS students and Cyber Security Engineering students. Students in Scientists and Engineers are required to take CSCI 402.

courses.name: Security Systems

courses.description: Linear programming models for resource allocation; simplex and revised simplex methods; duality; sensitivity; transportation problems; selected extensions to large scale, multiobjective and special structured models.
courses.name: Linear Programming and Extensions

courses.description: Introduction to key components of human language technologies, including: information extraction, sentiment analysis, question answering, machine translation.
courses.name: Applied Natural Language Processing

courses.description: Modern secret codes. Public key cryptosystems of Rivest- Shamir-Adleman, Diffie-Hellman and others. The underlying number theory and computational complexity theory.
courses.name: Introduction to Cryptography

courses.description: Computer architecture from a design perspective: Pipelined processors, speculative execution, VLIW, vector processors, GPU/GPGPU, memory technology and systems, interconnection networks, shared-memory and message-passing multiprocessors, chip multiprocessors.
courses.name: Computer Systems Architecture

courses.description: Foundations of symbolic intelligent systems, search, logic, knowledge representation, planning, learning.

sections.notes: On campus students must enroll in this quiz section.

courses.name: Foundations of Artificial Intelligence

courses.description: Deep learning research in computer vision, natural language processing and robotics; neural networks; deep learning algorithms, tools and software.
courses.recommendedPrep: CSCI 567, Python programming, calculus, linear algebra, probability and statistics, knowledge of machine learning

sections.description: Students who complete CSCI 527 WILL NOT RECEIVE DEGREE CREDIT for CSCI 566.

courses.name: Deep Learning and Its Applications

courses.description: Statistical methods for building intelligent and adaptive systems that improve performance from experiences; Focus on theoretical understanding of these methods and their computational implications. Recommended preparation: Undergraduate level training or coursework in linear algebra, multivariate calculus, basic probability and statistics; an undergraduate level course in Artificial Intelligence may be helpful but is not required.
courses.name: Machine Learning

courses.description: Explores fundamental techniques such as recursion, Fourier transform ordering, dynamic programming for efficient algorithm construction. Examples include arithmetic, algebraic, graph, pattern matching, sorting, searching algorithms.
courses.name: Analysis of Algorithms

courses.description: Study of client-side and server-side Web programming languages, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python, Java, Kotlin. Vibe coding with IDEs, AI Assistants and LLMs.
courses.recommendedPrep: knowledge of at least two programming languages.
courses.name: Web Technologies

courses.description: Examines key aspects of information retrieval as they apply to search engines; web crawling, indexing, querying and quality of results are studied.
courses.recommendedPrep: Familiarity in programming in multiple languages, C, C++, and/or Java and experience with a database
courses.name: Information Retrieval and Web Search Engines

courses.description: Course outlines the process of creating images from 3D models. Includes transformations, shading, lighting, rastorization, texturing, and other topics.
courses.name: 3-D Graphics and Rendering

courses.description: Database system architecture; conceptual database models; semantic, object-oriented, logic-based, and relational databases; user and program interfaces; database system implementation; integrity, security, concurrency and recovery. Recommended Preparation: Knowledge of relational databases, SQL, relational algebra and physical database design is required. Open only to graduate students.
courses.name: Database Systems

courses.description: Research leading to the master's degree. Maximum units which may be applied to the degree to be determined by the department. Graded CR/NC.

sections.notes: This section is for General Directed Research projects with faculty.

courses.name: Directed Research

courses.description: Exploration and critical assessment of research activities in computer science. Course will serve as a forum for current research presentations from academia and industry. Graded CR/NC.
courses.name: Computer Science Research Colloquium

courses.description: Credit on acceptance of thesis.  Graded IP/CR/NC.
courses.name: Master's Thesis

courses.description: Credit on acceptance of thesis.  Graded IP/CR/NC.
courses.name: Master's Thesis

courses.description: Credit on acceptance of thesis.  Graded IP/CR/NC.
courses.name: Master's Thesis

courses.description: Hands-on training on the basics of parallel computing and scientific visualization in the context of computer simulations in science and engineering. Recommended preparation: CSCI 455 and MATH 458 .
courses.name: Scientific Computing and Visualization

courses.description: Modern developments in data representation/analysis, nonconvex optimization, high-dimensional probability, sketching, clustering, low-rank models, inverse problems, applications in imaging, machine learning, computer vision and neuroscience.

sections.notes: For PhD students. However, MS students can request a waiver from the instructor in August if there is space available to enroll

courses.name: Mathematics of High-Dimensional Data

courses.description: Convex sets, functions, and optimization problems. Basic convex analysis and theory of convex programming. Novel, efficient first-order algorithms. Applications in the information and data sciences.
courses.name: Optimization for the Information and Data Sciences

courses.description: Guest speakers, workshops, presentations, discussion, challenges and opportunities of interdisciplinary research in operations research and artificial intelligence.

schoolRestrictions.groupDescription: Andrew and Erna Viterbi School of Engineering

courses.name: Seminar in Operations Research and Artificial Intelligence

courses.description: A unified treatment of data-driven decision-making for both single- and multi-agent systems from the perspectives of artificial intelligence and operations research.
courses.recommendedPrep: Optimization (at the level of CSCI 675 or ISE 630); machine learning and its coding in Python (at the level of CSCI 467, CSCI 567, or ISE 529); basic probability (at the level of ISE 220) and linear algebra (at the level of MATH 225 or MATH 235).
courses.name: Interdisciplinary Approaches to Data-Driven Decision-Making

courses.description: Use of analytics to address societal challenges arising in public health, social services and conservation; fairness, interpretability, robustness, optimization, machine learning and causal inference.
courses.recommendedPrep: Familiarity with modeling using integer optimization (e.g., at the level taught in ISE 631) and experience with ML using Python.
courses.name: Analytics for Social Impact

courses.description: Large-scale optimization algorithms that arise in machine learning: stochastic optimization, accelerated methods, parallelization, nonsmooth optimization, online optimization, variance reduction, min-max games.
courses.name: Large Scale Optimization and Machine Learning

courses.description: Fault models; test generation; fault simulation; self-checking and self-testing circuits; design for testability; fault tolerant design techniques; case studies; search techniques; memory testing.
courses.name: Diagnosis and Design of Reliable Digital Systems

courses.description: Fundamental techniques for design and analysis of algorithms. Dynamic programming; network flows; theory of NP-completeness; linear programming; approximation, randomized, and online algorithms; basic cryptography. Prerequisite: CSCI 570. Recommended preparation: familiarity with algorithms and discrete mathematics. Open only to doctoral students.
courses.name: Advanced Analysis of Algorithms

courses.description: Topics include: Convex sets and functions; convex optimization problems; geometric and Lagrangian duality; simplex algorithm; ellipsoid algorithm and its implications; matroid theory; submodular optimization.
courses.name: Convex and Combinatorial Optimization

courses.description: Fundamental issues in computer vision: theory, algorithms and applications. Image formation, image segmentation, inference and measurement of 3-D, motion analysis, object and activity recognition.
courses.recommendedPrep: Calculus, analytical geometry, linear algebra and probability theory. Programming in C or C++, data structures, algorithms
courses.name: Advanced Computer Vision

courses.description: The mathematical foundation of machine learning with a focus on algorithm design for sequential prediction problems.
courses.recommendedPrep: MATH 208x, MATH 225, MATH 125g, CSCI 570 and CSCI  567
courses.name: Theoretical Machine Learning

courses.description: Introduction of Ph.D. students to the broad range of computer science research. Two semesters registration required. Open to Computer Science doctoral students only. Graded CR/NC. Duplicates credit in former CSCI 597.
courses.name: Seminar in Computer Science Research

courses.description: Practical principles for the long-term development of effective teaching in Computer Science. Intended for teaching assistants for classes offered by the Computer Science department. Graded CR/NC. Open only to Computer Science doctoral students.
courses.name: Practicum in Teaching Computer Science

courses.description: Research leading to the doctorate. Maximum units which may be applied to the degree to be determined by the department. Graded CR/NC.
courses.name: Research
